title: Pleurothallis minor
taxonomy: Pleurothallis minor is a species of orchid in the genus Pleurothallis, which belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This family is further classified under the order Asparagales and the class Liliopsida. Pleurothallis minor is native to Central and South America and is a terrestrial orchid. The classification of Pleurothallis minor is a part of the larger system of plant taxonomy. The species name 'minor' suggests that it is a smaller species within the genus.

intro: Pleurothallis minor, commonly known as the Lesser Pleurothallis, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, this plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce symptoms of respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents responsible for these effects include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are present in the plant's leaves and flowers. In herbal medicine, Pleurothallis minor is often prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments, due to its purported ability to soothe skin irritations and promote tissue repair.
